entry.title: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content:
Hi,

I have following code:

 

for( int i = 0; i < 5; i++ )
{
catalog[i].TargetType = SQL_C_WCHAR;
catalog[i].BufferLength = ( bufferSize + 1 );
catalog[i].TargetValuePtr = malloc( sizeof( unsigned char ) * catalog[i].BufferLength );
ret = SQLBindCol( m_hstmt, (SQLUSMALLINT) i + 1, catalog[i].TargetType, catalog[i].TargetValuePtr, catalog[i].BufferLength, &( catalog[i].StrLen_or_Ind ) );
if( ret != SQL_SUCCESS && ret != SQL_SUCCESS_WITH_INFO )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg, STMT_ERROR );
result = 1;
break;
}
}

 

This code fails with HY090.

 

However it didn't fail with any other DBMS.

 

Im running SQL Anywhere 17.0 with the driver 17.0.

 

Any idea?

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: CDS : How to define Hierarchy View
entry.content:
Hi @LinaRaut ,
Need your expert help. 

We have sort of similar requirements but instead of having it in an analytical view, it needs to be consumed via Fiori List Report. And this is where my technical struggle comes in. 
After doing the 'Define Hierarchy...', in the consumption view, aside from providing the needed association to self, I've as well provided the annotation @OData.hierarchy.recursiveHierarchy: [{entity.name: <name of entity in Define Hierarchy>}]. 
But if I do this, I am encountering error: 'Description Resource Path Location Type
Error in entity 'ZTMP_C_GL_HIER2(CDS)': Hierarchy for view ZTMP_C_GL_HIER2 is not supported ZTMP_C_GL_HIER_SB (Service Binding) '


If I replace the 'entity.name' with parentNodeElement:  and nodeElement: option, I am not able the output in a Hierarchal format via preview in Service Binding. 
My codes as follows:
1) For DEFINE HIERARCHY

define hierarchy ztmp_gl_hier_def

as parent child hierarchy (

source ztmp_gl_hier

child to parent association _Parent

start where ParentNode is initial

siblings order by HierarchyNode ascending

)

{

key HierarchyNode,

ParentNode

}


2) For Consumption View:

@OData.hierarchy.recursiveHierarchy: [{

entity.name: 'ztmp_gl_hier_def'

}]

 

define view ztmp_c_gl_hier2 as select from ztmp_gl_hier

association [1..*] to ztmp_c_gl_hier2 as _Parent

on $projection.ParentNode = _Parent.HierarchyNode

{

@UI:{ selectionField: [{ position: 10 }],

lineItem: [{ position: 10 }] }

key GLAccountHierarchy,

@UI:{ selectionField: [{ position: 20 }],

lineItem: [{ position: 20 }] }

key HierarchyNode,

@UI: selectionField: [{ position: 40 }],

lineItem: [{ position: 40 }] }

ParentNode,

_Parent

}

 Much thanks.

entry.title: SAP PP | Looking for First Opportunity
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

My name is Fatima . 

For the past three years, I have been working on building myself in the SAP field, specifically in the MM & PP modules.

I have completed multiple internships and taken courses, but most of these experiences were focused only on learning, without leading to real opportunities afterward.

I believe I deserve a chance in a strong field like this. However, the environment I am in has made it difficult for me to move forward.

I would truly appreciate any  guidance.

Thank you all .

Looking forward to be part of the great learning.

entry.title: Re: A method for deploying made-to-order (MTO) items with...
entry.content: Thank you for your detailed response. In conclusion, for build-to-order items under policy 20, is it technically impossible to generate dependent requirements for component items, as well as finished goods, within the MRP area? Also, with policy 40, while individual segment requirements are not generated, we confirmed that order requirements are generated within the MRP area. Given this, is it possible to create and operate a new custom policy combining policies 20 and 40 through CBC customization changes? We understand that policy customization cannot be changed by the user via key configuration.

entry.title: Re: Onboarding 1.0 Decommissioning Procedure & FAQ
entry.content: @ThomasBilbaugh to the point above, will we still be able to extract data from 1.0 via API? Namely completed ONB forms? Thanks!

entry.title: Relationship between CBC tenant and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants - Is it 1:1 or 1:N?
entry.content:
**Title:**
Relationship between CBC tenant and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants - Is it 1:1 or 1:N?

**Body:**

Hello SAP Community,

I'm trying to understand the relationship between the CBC (Central Business Configuration) tenant and the various S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants throughout the implementation lifecycle.

**Background:**
From my understanding, the landscape includes the following tenants:

**During Starter Phase:**
- 1 CBC Tenant
- Starter Customizing Tenant (100)
- Starter Development Tenant (080)

**After 3-System Landscape Provisioning:**
- 1 CBC Tenant (same as above?)
- Development Customizing Tenant (100)
- Development Development Tenant (080)
- Test Tenant
- Production Tenant

**My Question:**
Is the CBC tenant relationship to these S/4HANA tenants **1:1** or **1:N**?

Specifically:
1. Does **one CBC tenant** manage configuration for **all five** S/4HANA tenants (Starter Customizing, Starter Development, Dev Customizing, Dev Development, Test, Production)?
2. Or does each S/4HANA tenant have its **own dedicated CBC tenant**?
3. When transitioning from Starter to the 3-system landscape, is it the same CBC tenant or a new one?

**Why this matters:**
I need to understand:
- Whether I create separate CBC projects within one CBC tenant for each target tenant
- Or if there are multiple CBC tenant instances
- How CBC orchestrates configuration across these six different tenants

Any clarification with documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026: What's New in Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
The 1H 2026 release brings 14 features to Employee Central Payroll, covering extensibility improvements, AI-powered pay statement capabilities, Payroll Control Center enhancements, expanded replication support, and legal changes across multiple countries and regions. Here is a detailed breakdown of every feature in this release.

Basic Extensibility for Infotype Actions (0000)

You can now use basic extensibility to replicate custom fields from Employee Central to the Actions infotype (0000) in Employee Central Payroll. Previously, achieving this required implementing the HRSFEC_B_CE_CHANGE_IT0000 BAdI or using advanced extensibility. With this release, you can configure custom field mappings through the standard configuration interface with no custom coding required.

For customers on HCM for SAP S/4HANA, this is particularly relevant because the HRSFEC_B_CE_CHANGE_IT0000 BAdI is listed as unsupported in the simplification list (SI35: HCM_ECP_BAdIs) and business impact note 3394928. Previously, using it required requesting an exception. This standard extensibility option removes that dependency entirely.

Type: New | Enablement: Customer configured

Prerequisite: Install the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Enhancements to Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities

The Payroll Control Center: Manage Payroll Activities solution receives several enhancements in this release. Note that this solution is only available in the SAP S/4HANA HCM (S4HCM101) system.

Archiving Function: You can now use the archiving object HR: Payroll Control Center Activity List (PCC_ACL) to archive the audit trail of payroll activity list recurrences. This integrates with SAP Information Lifecycle Management. The write program PYC_ARCHIVE_ACL_WRI reads audit trail data that has fulfilled the residency period and writes it to archive files. The read program PYC_ARCHIVE_ACL_READ reads the archived data.

Activity Audit Trail App: A new app lets you check logs for payroll activity list recurrences, activities, steps, and alerts.

Synchronous Update of Employee Selection: Employee data frequently changes during the payroll cycle due to organizational changes, transfers, terminations, and data corrections. These changes can cause stored employee selections in payroll activities to become outdated. The system now continuously monitors for such changes and automatically updates employee selections for ongoing activities. You can configure update frequency and scope, and the system provides notifications when manual review is recommended.

Role-Based Permission for Off-Cycle Payrolls: A new permission, Manage Off-Cycle Payrolls, controls access to the Manage Off-Cycle Payrolls app.

Transport Request for Plan Payroll Activities: A new checkbox in General Settings in the Admin Transaction Report (PYT_ADMIN_TRANSACTION) requires users to specify a transport request when creating activity list recurrences.

Type: Changed | Classification: Major | Enablement: Customer configured

Prerequisite: Install the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Enhancements to AI-Assisted Explain My Pay Statement

Joule-powered pay statement explanations are now available for two additional countries. Employees in Brazil can use Joule in Portuguese, and employees in Canada can use it in English and French. Employees can ask Joule to explain terms or amounts on their most recent pay statement or compare it with the previous statement. Explanations include wage type names or wage type information from the payroll result, depending on the document grounding configuration.

Previously, this feature was available for Australia (English), Canada (French), Germany (German), Mexico (Spanish), Netherlands (Dutch), United Kingdom (English), and United States (English). The additions in this release are Brazil (Portuguese) and Canada (English).

Type: Changed | Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

Prerequisites: AI Units license, Joule enabled for SAP SuccessFactors, Explain Pay option enabled in AI Services Administration, pay statement (direct) integration enabled, and the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Enhanced Gender Field Replication

Employee Central Payroll now supports replication of Male and Female values from the country/region-specific gender field in Employee Central Global Information for all countries. Previously, replication from this field only worked for countries mapped in SAP's standard table (T77PAD_GENDER). For countries without mappings, customers had to use the standard gender field, which meant updating gender in two places.

With this change, Male and Female values are accepted for any country, even without a mapping in T77PAD_GENDER. Broader gender values such as non-binary are still replicated only for countries with SAP-delivered mappings. Unsupported values for unmapped countries will be blocked with an error message.

Type: Changed | Action: Required | Enablement: Automatically on

Prerequisite: Enable the country/region-specific gender field in Manage Business Configuration and assign a picklist that includes Male and Female values.

Employee Central Payroll User ID Generation from Identity Authentication Service

You can now generate the EC Payroll user ID from the external name provided by the Identity Authentication Service system. This works with EC Payroll on both SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP HCM. The system maps the Login Name in the Identity Provisioning Service to create the EC Payroll User ID. This decouples employee data replication from user synchronization, providing more flexibility in the identity management process.

Type: New | Enablement: Automatically on

Global User ID Supported for SAML Authentication

You can now use Global User ID as the primary identifier for SAML authentication in Employee Central Payroll based on SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA. This ensures compliance with identity and access management standards while improving integration capabilities.

Note that this feature is available only for new customers of EC Payroll based on SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA.

Type: New | Enablement: Customer configured

Replication of Start and End Times in Time Valuation Results

Start and end times in time valuation results are now replicated to the Employee Remuneration Info (2010) infotype. You can configure time valuation results in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking to include start times, end times, and a previous-day indicator, which are then replicated to the payroll system using the point-to-point integration.

This supports country-specific payroll requirements where start and end times are needed for accurate processing of wage types such as night premiums, Sunday premiums, and public holiday premiums. For example, German regulations under Section 3b of the Einkommensteuergesetz (Income Tax Law) require this information.

Type: New | Enablement: Customer configured

Prerequisite: Install the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Country-Specific Replication Enhancements

Several replication enhancements target specific countries in this release:

Norway: Work Capacity Percentage. The Available Work Capacity Percentage field can now be replicated from Employee Central to the Absences infotype (2001), field PSARB in EC Payroll. This field is used to calculate payments for employees unable to work at full capacity due to illness.

Sweden: Work Incapacity Percentage. Similarly, the Work Incapacity Percentage field is now replicated to the Absences infotype (2001) for Sweden, supporting accurate payment processing for partial absences due to illness.

Italy: Sickness Certificate Identifier. A new 20-character alphanumeric field for the sickness certificate identifier in the country-specific Time Off object for Italy is now replicated to the UMSCH field in the Absences infotype (2001). This value is included in the monthly UniEMENS report sent to INPS (the Italian national security body). Note that only absences created as of March 2026 can be replicated.

United Kingdom: Student Loan Plan Type 5. A new field for Student Loan Plan Type 5 from the Starter Checklist onboarding form can now be replicated to Tax Data GB (0065) and Court Orders GB (0070) infotypes.

New Zealand: KiwiSaver Onboarding Fields. The Savings Suspension End Date and Temporary Rate Reduction End Date fields from Section A of the KiwiSaver (KS2) onboarding form are now replicated to EC Payroll.

Canada: TD1 Compliance Forms. Canadian onboarding compliance form data (TD1 and province-specific variants) can now be automatically replicated from Onboarding to EC Payroll. This covers fields from infotype 0463 (Federal Tax CA), infotype 0461 (Tax Assignment CA), and infotype 0462 (Provincial Tax CA).

All replication enhancements require the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026.

Legal Changes for Country/Region Specifics

The 1H 2026 release includes the following legal changes:

Country Description SAP Note(s)
EgyptIntegration of payroll with Egypt Tax Authority System (Unified Tax Procedure)3645008
SwitzerlandYear-end legal changes 2025/20263645577, 3706563, 3708154, 3703095, 3703501, 3657336, 3657337
PortugalLaw No. 104/2019: Reform of SIOE for Public Sector Reporting3701490, 3694988, 3700601, 3694334, 3690959, 3693907
NetherlandsYear-end legal changes 2025/20263664733, 3664710
GermanyIntroduction of KEA basic service for reduced hours compensation3602880
GermanyEEL new dataset version January 1, 20263634007
New ZealandEI Return Validations retrospective processing changes3693533
SingaporeMigration of IRAS IR21-API to Government's APEX3610023
BrazilLaw 15.270/2025: Withhold Income Tax (IRRF) Reduction3684446, 3687982, 3687983
TurkeySGK Min Wage and TAX Brackets changes for in-month changes3609544
United KingdomStatutory Parental Bereavement Pay updates for Northern Ireland, April 20263658742, 3681863, 3681864, 3693132, 3693133, 3693134

For more details on any legal change, use the Regulatory Change Manager: choose View All, then All Regulatory Changes, and filter by SAP SuccessFactors under Product and the relevant country.

Type: New | Classification: Major | Enablement: Customer configured

Important Note on Support Packages

Most features in this release require the Synchronization HR Support Package delivered in May 2026. Until this support package is released, the features cannot be fully used. The schedules are published separately for SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP HCM (EA-HR EHP8). Plan your upgrade accordingly.

Summary

Here is a quick reference of all 14 EC Payroll features in the 1H 2026 release:

Feature Type Action Enablement
Basic Extensibility for Infotype 0000NewInfo onlyCustomer configured
Payroll Control Center EnhancementsChangedInfo onlyCustomer configured
AI-Assisted Explain My Pay Statement (Brazil, Canada)ChangedInfo onlyContact CEE/AM
Enhanced Gender Field ReplicationChangedRequiredAutomatically on
EC Payroll User ID from Identity AuthNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Global User ID for SAML AuthNewInfo onlyCustomer configured
Start/End Times in Time Valuation to IT2010NewInfo onlyCustomer configured
Work Capacity Replication for NorwayNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Work Incapacity Replication for SwedenNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Sickness Certificate Replication for ItalyChangedInfo onlyAutomatically on
Student Loan Plan Type 5 for UKChangedInfo onlyAutomatically on
KiwiSaver Fields for New ZealandChangedInfo onlyAutomatically on
TD1 Compliance Forms for CanadaNewInfo onlyAutomatically on
Legal Changes for Country/Region SpecificsNewInfo onlyCustomer configured

Preview instances have features available as of April 13, 2026. Production availability is May 15, 2026. All features with replication dependencies require the May 2026 Synchronization HR Support Package.

Pay particular attention to the Enhanced Gender Field Replication feature, which carries a Required action classification and is Automatically On. Review your gender field configuration to ensure your picklists are set up correctly before the production release. 

This post is based on the publicly available SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 What's New documentation. Always refer to the official SAP documentation for implementation decisions.

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
Hi,

I was trying the Challenge 1 but in the last step I get the below error:

Stream the response, I get error as OrchestrationError: 400 - Grounding Module: Document Grounding retrieval API returned: One or more collections could not be found: ['Collection d7834fe4-83f0-4d6e-8583-1be833a2ca0b not found']

Is DATA_REPOSITORY_ID = "d7834fe4-83f0-4d6e-8583-1be833a2ca0b"
 
Regards,
Nikhil

entry.title: Could calendar configuration transport to Q & P tenant by change request？
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

When i made calendar configuration in the Customizing system, it didn't generate the change request automatically.

Could calendar configuration transport to Q & P tenant by change request？

Or could i make calendar configuration directly in Q & P tenant ？

Thanks

Ying

entry.title: Can not use default value function for Sales Contract type
entry.content: My users need a default value for the Sales Contract Type field when creating a sales contract. Although they have maintained the value in the Default Values settings, the field remains empty when using the Manage Sales Contracts app. Other fields, such as Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, and Division, are working as expected

entry.title: Re: Handling Dynamic Fields in SAP RAP using JSON Payload in CDS Custom Entity
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

In contrast to Standard CDS Views:

  • Custom Entities enable ABAP's dynamic data processing.
  • When logic cannot be managed only at the database level, they are usually employed.
  • They offer the freedom to execute intricate or unconventional scenarios, including external integrations or dynamic queries.

Because the data is fetched and processed in the ABAP layer, Custom Entities are well-suited for scenarios where:

  • Input parameters need to be interpreted dynamically
  • Data sources or structures vary at runtime
  • Standard CDS capabilities are not sufficient

"

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing again, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: ADOBE LIFECYCLE DESIGNER
entry.content: Hi Ranjan, is it possible for you to reupload the app? The link is broken Thanks in advance!!

entry.title: Re: 1 warehouse order for outbound with two activity areas
entry.content: Thanks for this information, it helped me alot!

entry.title: Re: ILM Store Configuration For Azure Blob
entry.content: This is indeed detailed and helpful information - thank you for sharing

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content:
I don't see anything specific in this code snippet nor can I reproduce the error in an more complete implementation. I would suggest getting an ODBC trace. In that trace, please ensure that the SQL_ATTR_ODBC_VERSION is set to SQL_OV_ODBC3 (SQLSetEnvAttr)  and then look for the 

DIAG [HY090] [Microsoft][ODBC Driver Manager] Invalid string or buffer length (0)

to confirm what ODBC function encounters the error. A call to SQLBindCol has a context for the error related to the BufferLength being not negative.  If it is coming from a different ODBC function, share what that function is and how your application has implemented it.

This really does not appear to be a SQLA driver issue. If you can share the trace, we can use ODBCTest to replay the ODBC calls leading to the error.

entry.title: Re: Managing Goal Libraries - How to add other languages...
entry.content: @SOFIAANNA_VAMVOUKLI  Reason is the goal library for other Language is not created in system ,once you create goal library for respective language they will start appearing ,below is the sample for french library . please mark this as answer if this helps so that others can take benefit from this response

entry.title: Re: How to redefine RDS based OData services?
entry.content: @Andre_Fischer using this method we can expose custom CDS view's how about exposing additional associations, added an associations to one of the standard view in the same service which is already exposed, but when following approach this association is not exposed.

entry.title: Re: My SAP Business AI Certification Journey
entry.content: Excellent Blog

entry.title: Re: How can I connect my SAP BTP Kyma with my On-Premise...
entry.content: Hi @Aji , Good Day. Resurrecting this after a year as we have stumbled at the same roadblock. This link redirects us to the the connectivity proxy. However, there's no much documentation towards extending JCO to be used in the Kyma runtime. There's no specification on how the SAPJCO calls need to be routed via the connectivity proxy. Nor does SAPJCO seem to natively support proxying. We have been able to proxy the requests using tools like nginx's tcp routing but are stuck with connectivity-proxy throwing error "com.sap.core.connectivity.tunnel.server.proxy.InvalidHandshakePropertiesException: Destination host name not set " even though SAP JCO passes the "ashost" parameter. That 's the closest we have gotten.  We know the code works as it is successful on premise. We have also tried with hardcoding the destination parameters and at the same time configuring the destination as an RFC destination and using the SAP Cloud SDK to fetch destination details. FYI : We seem to have all the other components setup correctly eg: Cloud Connector with connectivity to on-premise , Connectivity Proxy module in Kyma, RFC destination.  We are reaching a point where we might need to consider a change in architecture but we would prefer not to if possible. Something like Kyma - HTTP -> Cloud Connector - HTTP -> Some new custom App in CloudFoundry on the lines on https://github.com/skye0402/jco-rfc-rest-server  - RFC -> Connectivity Service -> Cloud Connector -> On Prem . Since there seems to be a lot more doco for Cloud Foundry to on prem communication via RFC.  If you or any other Kyma experts on the forum have any guidance towards how to proceed invoking RFC protocol'd function modules using SAPJCO, it would be most appreciated. We suspect that its some configuration that we are missing or its just that SAP JCO is not supported or we are just integrating the wrong components together.

entry.title: Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Week 2 completed. UserID : AC300864U247

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer error in SOIC sten
entry.content:
The issue is not because of stock but pricing. Perform the below steps

  1. Run transaction VA01 manually to create a sales order for the same customer/material and check pricing.
  2. Use Pricing Analysis (button in sales order) to see which condition types are missing.
  3. Maintain missing condition records in VK11.
  4. Once pricing is correct, the background process will generate the sales order successfully.

entry.title: Re: 《Success Guide》：助力 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 项目顺利实施的实战宝典
entry.content: 非常有帮助的文档😍

entry.title: Re: Assignment of incoterm locations in SAP TM
entry.content:
Hi Micheal, 

Thanks for sharing.  do we have a case where same incoterm location text is used by 2 different incoterms? if so how to manage? 

Thanks,

Archana

entry.title: Re: Can not use default value function for Sales Contract...
entry.content: The system does not support defaulting the Sales Contract Type field in Public Cloud. Having said that, it is not a configuration error on your side. Users will need to select it manually or use templates/variants as a workaround until SAP enables defaulting for this field.

entry.title: Re: EWM stock backdate
entry.content: Standard SAP does not provide backdated bin-level stock reporting. You’ll need to rely on material document history (standard reports like MB51 or MB5B)  for legal purposes or build a custom snapshot/reporting solution if bin-level detail is mandatory.

entry.title: Re: Sales Tax Calculation for Free Item and Tax amount to...
entry.content:
Spoiler
We tried to achieve by applying standard 100% discount condition after calculation of tax at item level. But this is driving the Order Item Net value (negative) -5 with Tax amount as 5. This cannot be submitted to GST.

From your above statement I believe, the step number for the above is including the tax condition type step number.  Just set the Step number of From-To of pr00 (that is only the price) to this 100% discount condition type, so that it will not impact the tax

entry.title: Re: Sourcing & Procurement in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
Excellent summary, packed with valuable insights — it has been incredibly helpful to me.😁

@kuoli_jing

entry.title: Re: SAP Partner: SAP Cloud ERP Implementation Tools Overview
entry.content: Great summary with lots of useful takeaways. 🏆 This was really helpful for me.

entry.title: Re: Need Solution to Ignore Available Stock for Dependent...
entry.content: Apart from storage location exclusion, the cleanest standard option is to use MRP areas or special procurement keys. If you need stricter control, you’ll have to use a Cloud BAdI enhancement to enforce procurement proposals irrespective of stock.

entry.title: Re: Error When Making an HTTP GET Call to a REST Endpoint
entry.content: Do you have any docu for the API to double check expected format? Maybe dateTimeStart="2026-04-13T15:13:14" or dateTimeStart='2026-04-13T15:13:14' to set it as string would already work?

entry.title: Re: Sales Tax Calculation for Free Item and Tax amount to...
entry.content: Thanks for your quick response. But the business requirement is to give discount including the Selling Amount + Tax to customer.

entry.title: EWM - Modifying custom fields in HU header using fm /SCWM/HUHDR_ATTR_CHANGE sometimes does not work
entry.content:
Hi, EWM/ABAP experts.

Good day! 
Need your help for some technical query please.
I need to understand how function module /SCWM/HUHDR_ATTR_CHANGE works.

I need to update an HU during Goods Receipt of an Inbound Delivery via Warehouse Monitor (/SCWM/MON) in an EWM system. The BADI we found for this is /SCWM/IF_EX_DLV_GM_PROCESS, method CHECK_DOCUMENT. We've tested it and it works, we are able to persist data in our custom fields in HU header table.

Now the problem is sometimes it does not work.
In this particular instance, I was trying to post GR to update an HU like normal, but even though we're getting the values correctly, the database is not getting updated at all.


I have a theory that Update Task is the culprit, but I am not very familiar with the Update Task feature of ABAP.  This is because when I enabled Update debugging, a debugger session popped out saying that the changes in the FM might be overwritten.

So my main questions are:

  1. How does fm /SCWM/HUHDR_ATTR_CHANGE work? Does it change the HU tables directly, or it's just putting the changes in a buffer?
  2. Can another process like Update Task override changes in HU header maintenance during the same runtime, even without errors?

Thanks for your help!

entry.title: Re: Seamless Authentication to Secured ArcGIS Online Services from SAP Spatial Asset Management
entry.content:
Hi Aneesh,

Thanks for this blog.

You have mentioned that “You also need to set up the redirection URL as the URL of SAP Spatial Asset Management while configuring the ArcGIS Online application.”
Could you please help me with the actual URL? Currently, we can access all Spatial Asset Management–related applications through the SAP Fiori Launchpad, but we do not have a specific standalone URL for SAP Spatial Asset Management.
Alternatively, could you please guide me on where and how to obtain the correct redirect URL?
I am getting the below error:

Regards,

Shirsendu Roy

entry.title: Prevent Dynamic Break being applied for Absences - Time Tracking 1H 2026
entry.content:
Is there a way at all to prevent Dynamic Break from adding a break when full day absences are being requested? I was hoping the 1H 2026 feature of "Dynamic Break handling" field on Time type will solve for this but looks like it isnt.

The guide says : "For full day absences, the break is generated in the time sheet irrespective of the time type setting".

Is there any way of modifying this behavior?

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign SAP AI Core Service in BTP Trial for...
entry.content:
Hi Rabi,

SAP's AI Core Service, along with a number of other service are not available for a BTP Trial account, despite being marked as "Free Tier". You would require your own non-trial account to access the free tier service. 

You can check out this document for additional information!

Warm regards,

Guruprasad GL

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign SAP AI Core Service in BTP Trial for...
entry.content: Hi GuruprasadGL! Thanks for your comment. But why is it asked for my to preform such task in the lesson of the learning journey without telling me that I need another access? Or is it that I have the access with my learning hub subscription but haven’t fully activated it? Because if you were to fully follow the steps you are asked to an SAP AI CORE in BTP. I feel like if I can’t accomplish this task I don’t know what to expect if I go on with the lesson and the whole learning journey.

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content: @chris_keating, I generated the log file. Please do let me know how and where to upload/send it. This site interface is not designed to do that. Thx.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign SAP AI Core Service in BTP Trial for...
entry.content: Hi GuruprasadGL! Firstly I accidentally accepted the solution without trying it out. Secondly my question is how come the lesson in the learning journey ask me to perform such task when I cant? Or is it expected that I should be able but haven't been able to fully do it with my learning hub subscription?

entry.title: Re: Adding a new Test client after transport released
entry.content:
Good morning, 

Really this is more to TMS experts.

However, I would say that for the transport already released, if you add a new client in the transport group later on, still you need to add this transport manually to the import buffer of that additional system:client aded after the release of the transport.

It is at release time when the transport are being added to the import buffer of the systems:clients indicated in the TMS transport route. Only then the trigger of the import from the Feature can be succesful.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to export all the information within B...
entry.content:
Share my CDS Views as below for your reference.

=====================================================

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZVPP_IT_STAS'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ZVPP_IT_STAS GET U2D'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view ZVPP_IT_STAS_HDB as select from stas
{
stlty,
stlnr,
stlal,
stlkn,
max(stasz) as stasz_m,
max(lkenz) as lkenz_m,
max(stvkn) as stvkn_m
}
group by
stlkn,
stlty,
stlnr,
stlal

=====================================================

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZVPP101'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Retrieve Bills of Material'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view ZVPP101_HDB as select from mkal as n

inner join mast as m
on n.matnr = m.matnr
and n.werks = m.werks
and n.stlan = m.stlan
and n.stlal = m.stlal

inner join stko as k
on m.stlnr = k.stlnr
and m.stlal = k.stlal
and m.stlan = '1'

inner join stpo as p
on k.stlty = p.stlty
and k.stlnr = p.stlnr

inner join ZVPP_IT_STAS_HDB as s
on k.stlty = s.stlty
and k.stlnr = s.stlnr
and k.stlal = s.stlal
and p.stlkn = s.stlkn
and not s.lkenz_m = 'X'

inner join mara as a
on a.matnr = n.matnr

inner join mara as b
on b.matnr = p.idnrk

left outer join I_ProductPlant as w
on p.idnrk = w.Product
and m.werks = w.Plant

{
a.mtart,
n.matnr,
a.matkl as matkl_m,
n.werks,
n.verid,
n.adatu as datuv,
n.bdatu as valid_to,
k.stlty,
k.stlnr,
k.stlal,
k.bmeng,
k.bmein,
p.stlkn,
p.posnr,
p.idnrk,
b.matkl as matkl_i,
p.postp,
p.potx1,
p.potx2,
p.menge,
p.meins,
p.fmeng,
p.matkl,
w.MaintenanceStatusName as pstat,
w.ProcurementType as beskz,
w.SpecialProcurementType as sobsl,
p.alpgr,
p.alprf
}

entry.title: SAP FSM unable to read Spatial data using API call
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using Service Maps in Field Service Management and leveraging spatial data to define regions. We can create this spatial data using an API. However, while attempting to read the spatial data using the standard API, the call is successful, but it returns null. We need to retrieve the latitude and longitude for this region. Any relevant input would be greatly appreciated.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_spatial_data_api/read-spatial-record.html

Thanks in advance.

Vignesh

entry.title: Re: Error When Making an HTTP GET Call to a REST Endpoint
entry.content: Hi Maik, Thank you for your response. Yes, we do have the API documentation, and the date format we are sending follows the API specification. If we use any other format, the call fails with a bad request error.

entry.title: Re: ODBC failure on basic call
entry.content: The code Im trying comes straight from https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/odbc/reference/syntax/sqltables-function?view=sql-server-ver17#example..

entry.title: Re: Migrating to optimised stories
entry.content: Once the classic stack deprecation enforced, all classic artifacts will be invisible from both public folder and private folder.

entry.title: Re: Valuated Assembly Item Usage Effects on CO FI
entry.content:
As you might be knowing, Settlement rules in PS are designed for cost objects like WBS elements, orders, or assets; not for inventory valuation. That is why CJ88 won’t settle to a material master.

Why this happens is because of the following:

1) Standard Price Materials
Costs are not adjusted by settlement; variances are posted to price difference accounts.

2) Network Activities
They collect costs (raw material consumption, activity confirmations), but settlement to a material is not supported.

3) Assembly Processing in PS
Designed more for project-driven procurement/assembly than for production costing.

If your client insists on standard price, you must accept that variances will be posted to price difference accounts, not to the material. If they want actual costs reflected in material valuation, they should use moving average price for those semi-finished/finished goods.

In practice, most buy-and-sell clients using PS for assembly adopt MAP for assembled items to keep valuation aligned with actual costs. Standard price is kept for stable, high-volume items.

entry.title: Re: Overhead costing plan/actual with engineering to orde...
entry.content: In Engineer-to-Order (ETO) with Project System, you can allocate overheads like electricity to networks using Costing Sheet / Template Allocation (CPT) functionality. Having said that you don’t manually enter electricity costs per activity. Instead, you configure a costing sheet and run CPT so the system allocates overheads proportionally to your network activities (e.g., based on labor hours or machine time). This keeps your ETO projects consistent with Controlling rules and ensures overheads are captured correctly.

entry.content:

Simulation trace is not showing any data. Please guide further.

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 Product Release LRN-184427 Will this finally...
entry.content:
Hi @Julie_Davis_Wyn 

To disable this warning message, try disabling the property "enableOnlineContentEarlyLaunchWarning" within LMS_ADMIN to see if it resolves the matter.

entry.title: Re: BADI to implement delivery split based on Stock Trans...
entry.content:
Good day @giusy1 

Thank you for your question.

The short answer to your question is NO, the Public Cloud does not provide a BAdI that adds new delivery split logic, which is based on the previous document item, such as splitting by Stock Transportation Order line Item during Schedule Delivery Creation (VL10B). The reason is that the Public Cloud BAdI catalog for Delivery Management exposes LE_SHP_SUPPRESS_DEL_SPLIT, which is specifically for supressing a standard delivery split, not for introducing a new custom possibility to enable you split.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/aff0b3f5f46c42a2b2c0fabfc233bab2/78ac6e6632494a8dbc0a63c8b63f6d77.html?locale=en-US

So, that means:

  • Standard delivery creation will split when standard delivery header criteria differ
  • There is a Public Cloud BAdI to suppress a split
  • The Public Cloud does not offer a BAdI to force an additional custom split by STO items or previous document line item.

If your business requires separate deliveries, the approach supported is to drive a standard delivery split through standard split relevant fields, not through a custom BAdI that splits from STO item.

Also, SAP provides functionality to split an outbound delivery afterward, but that is a separate action and not the same as custom split logic during initial collective creation.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Required BADI For Billing Document VF01 For Validatio...
entry.content: Hi Sir, Mentioned BADI - SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER not working is there any another way to restrict the Billing document at the time of Creation using T-code VF01. Regards Ashish

entry.title: Re: Looking for Reliable and Updated Practice Questions f...

entry.title: Re: Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud Classic Stories & Analytic Applications
entry.content:
@NPH 

I saw in a mail to SAC tenant admins following statements

  • Existing assets that are in the Classic Experiences (Stories, Analytical Applications, Data Analyzer, or Digital Boardroom) must be migrated before the 2026 Q3 QRC release
  • There are no exceptions to have access to the Classic Experience beyond the 2026 Q3 QRC

So I guess they will really disappear in Q3 2026 (around 14-16th of August in prod tenants)

entry.title: Re: WiP in Financial Accounting is still to be cleared/se...
entry.content: Not sure in which version you are working.  Check SAP note 170927 which is applicable to lower versions.

entry.title: Re: Hide Asset Class for Company Code or Country
entry.content:
Good day @joao_rosa51 

Thank you for your question.

The short answer to your question is, the Public Cloud does not offer a standard supported way for you to hide selected pre delivered asset classes from the asset class value help.  See KBAs 2747303 and 3348903 for more insights. So, it is safe to say:

  • No standard feature to selectively hide only some SAP delivered asset classes in the value help.
  • SAP delivered asset classes can remain visible once the company code is properly prepared for Asset Accounting.
  • Your current attempts with valuation view dependent settings are not the right lever to help you hide in the standard app.

So, asset class visibility in the Manage Fixed Asset app is governed first by Asset Account readiness for the company code, especially the takeover date and LDT segment setup in Make Company Code settings- Asset Account-Specific. If those are incomplete, only some asset classes appear, or asset creation is blocked.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/3781c5d2efea4956b4896d728916ad78.html?locale=en-US

To directly answer your question “Is there any way to satisfy this request in SAP Public Cloud?”

Answer: No, not as a standard hiding feature in Public Cloud.

The closest standard alternative in Public Cloud is not to hide the asset classes, but to control allowed usage at creation time. You can use the Manage Substitution and Validation Rules - For Asset Master Data app. Based on that; the clean core direction is for you to use a validation rule to prevent creating with disallowed asset classes for the relevant company code.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: PO Approval Templates in SAP B1
entry.content: Send us your query. Cheers, Andy

entry.title: Re: Enabling External Workflows for Documents in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: HI @Gairik , the full traceability in SAP Cloud ALM is planed for the future (who, when including comment and possibly also digital signature soon). Today this information is stored and available directly in BTP BPA. Best Regards.

entry.title: Re: Negative Stock in Transit (MB5T) after STO Reversal –...
entry.content:
Why it happens is because, the PGI was reversed but the GR remains and hence, the system sees more receipts than issues → negative SIT. GR reversal is blocked because the posting period is closed.
The standard corrective approach is to post a current-period manual adjustment (goods issue or inventory adjustment) in the receiving plant to bring SIT back to zero. This keeps financials intact and clears MB5T without reopening prior fiscal years.

entry.title: Re: Enabling External Workflows for Documents in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello @NagaSiva_CALM 

Sorry for the late reply, I hope your issue is corrected already by the customer message. In general, you can use the two roles (owner and responsible) for the approval notification but also specify other roles in the BTP BPA and notify them directly from there. The Workflows we provided are just examples how the functionality can be used. I hope this helps. Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Migrating to optimised stories
entry.content:
@NPH 

Post deprecation you would not be able to see the classic stories at all. Since you have already migrated to Optimized story over a course of time. Just use guidance in the below sap note to get list of all stories on your tenant and it will be categorized as classic or Optimized, so that you find the stories that got missed for the migration.

3652956 - How to extract a complete list of all stories including their dependencies and version information in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)? - SAP for Me

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Could calendar configuration transport to Q & P tenan...
entry.content:
Hello @_yingbirong76d707 ,

here is a link to the same question and an answer 🙂

https://community.sap.com/t5/cloud-erp-public-value-map-q-a/factory-calendar-can-not-be-transmitted-to-q-tenant-or-p-tenant/qaq-p/14356493

 

👉  "To transport customizations to the factory calendar from the Customizing tenant, you need to return to the overview after saving your customizations and click the “Distribute Data” button there.

A new or updated entry will then appear in the corresponding software collection."

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: DRC Reporting: Withholding Tax
entry.content:
Good day @isabel_martingomez,

what challenges are your facing exactly?

Kindly elaborate.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Extract report on GL line item with its total GL acco...
entry.content:
Good day @Liyana 

Thank you for your question.

In Display Line Items in General Ledger, the grouping you apply in the UI is a display grouping for analysis in the app. The Public Cloud does not offer a standard function to expand all groups at once or export the file while preserving the G/L account group hierarchy exactly as shown on screen.

So, i suggest you to:

  • Handle the Excel export as a flat line item export
  • The on-screen grouping is primarily for interactive analysis
  • The Public Cloud does not provide a standard Expand All function for the grouped rows in this app.

If your requirement is to extract a report per G/L account group together with its line items, the more suitable standard option is usually a statutory or dedicated report output, not the interactive grouped view export.

So, in practice:

  • Use Display Line Items in General Ledger for online analysis and drilldown
  • Use Run Statutory Reports --> G/L Account Line Items when you need a downloadable report format for reporting purposes.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to export Data from Datasphere to MS SQL Server
entry.content: any updates on this request?

entry.title: Re: Emergency contact mandatory rule
entry.content:
Thank you to the person who shared the answer to this question, much appreciated.

For anyone struggling to get it to work, make sure you complete this step after creating the message definition:
Note: please go to External User Visibility and run for MessageDefinition if new Message Definition is used in the rule

Search External User Visibility > Tick MessageDefinition > Run > Wait a couple of minutes then test again.

entry.title: Re: How can I set up fields for TmSp_GetDefaultElementsfo...

a) Only manually

b) There is no way to automatically transfer the texts along with their translations. If you only want to import the translations, then you need to use DTW with OMLT and MLT1 templates. However, there are no DTW templates for the OMLP table.

Please vote on this Customer Influence request:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/249350/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: Quiz Template Style Not Applying in Learner View After Publishing
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using SAP Enable Now  and facing an issue with quiz templates. When I apply a custom quiz template in Manager, it looks correct in the authoring view while previewing it. But after publishing, the Learner View falls back to the default player skin and the custom styling is lost.

Is there a way to deploy a custom quiz template style to the Work Center Learner View and  can it be done from Manager/Producer?

Any guidance would be appreciated. Thank you.

@ShaneLipke SAP Enable Now  SAP Enable Now, cloud edition

entry.title: Managing Multi-Country/Region Implementations with a Parallel Line
entry.content:
Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition across multiple countries/regions is a complex endeavor that requires careful planning and robust configuration management. The parallel line provides a powerful solution for managing multi-wave, multi-country/region rollouts without disrupting your productive environment. In this post, I’ll walk you through a proven approach for leveraging the parallel line to run phased country/region implementations efficiently and safely.

For more information about the system landscape with a parallel line, parallel line management, and the lifecycle processes branch, rebase, and merge, refer to the product assistance on SAP Help Portal.

Why Use a Parallel Line for Multi-Country/Region Implementations?

The parallel line offers a separate workspace in SAP Central Business Configuration and additional SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenants in your development and test systems. This separation enables you to:

  • Develop and test new country/region configurations without affecting your stable main line
  • Maintain operational continuity in live countries/regions while preparing for future waves
  • Validate complex configurations thoroughly before merging them into your main line
  • Reduce risk by isolating configuration changes until they're fully tested

Understanding the Foundation: The 5-Country/Region Limit

SAP Central Business Configuration has a critical deployment constraint that directly impacts how you plan and run multi-country/region implementations. The 5-country/region limit is a technical recommendation designed to optimize system performance and ensure smooth activation processes in the main line as well as the parallel line.

When the Limit Applies

This limit applies every time there is deployment and activation from SAP Central Business Configuration to the customizing tenant of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

  • For each group or batch of countries/regions implemented in the main line and deployed from the SAP Central Business Configuration workspace to the customizing tenant (D.100) in the main line
  • For each group or batch of countries/regions implemented in the parallel line and deployed from the SAP Central Business Configuration workspace to the parallel customizing tenant (D.120) in the parallel line

Critically for merges: When merging from the parallel line to the main line, deployment and activation are performed in one step. In this case, 5 is the maximum number for the entire wave, not just for a single configuration group or batch

This deliberate technical limit is designed to prevent performance bottlenecks, maintain system responsiveness, and ensure smooth configuration processes during critical implementation milestones.

How the Limit Works with the Merge Strategy

When you plan to merge configurations from the parallel line to the main line, you can scope a maximum of 5 countries/regions total in the parallel line before deploying them from the SAP Central Business Configuration workspace to the parallel customizing tenant (D.120).

Key Understanding: The 5-country/region limit becomes a total limit for the entire wave when you plan to merge. This is not just a per-batch limit. It’s the maximum you can merge from the parallel line to the main line in one implementation wave.

Why the 5-Country/Region Limit Matters

The recommended batch of 5 countries/regions serves three critical purposes that directly impact your implementation success:

  • Content Deployment Optimization: Large country/region volumes can strain content activation and transport processes, potentially causing failures or excessive processing times that disrupt project timelines. By limiting batches to 5 countries/regions, you ensure that the activation process completes reliably and within acceptable timeframes.
  • System Performance: Processing too many countries/regions simultaneously can create bottlenecks that affect system responsiveness during deployment and potentially impact other users working in the system. The 5-country/region limit prevents these performance degradations, ensuring consistent system behavior throughout your implementation.
  • Configuration Efficiency: Smooth processing during scope confirmation milestones reduces project risks by making each deployment cycle predictable and manageable. When deployments consistently succeed without performance issues, your project team can focus on configuration quality rather than troubleshooting technical problems.

Critical constraint: The limit becomes absolute during merge operations, where all parallel line configurations must be processed in a single wave.

Implementing the Multi-Wave Approach

The parallel line approach allows teams to work on new country/region configurations without disrupting live operations or ongoing customer adaptations in the main line. Here’s how to implement this strategy effectively:

Wave 1: Initial Implementation in the Main Line

Start by implementing your first group of countries/regions directly in the main line. Complete all configuration, testing, and validation activities, then proceed with your wave 1 go-live. This establishes your baseline configuration and validates your core setup.

Wave 2: Branch the Main Line and Configure in the Parallel Line

Step 1: Branch the Main Line to Set Up the Parallel Line

After wave 1 is live and stable, branch the parallel line through SAP for Me. This process replicates the main line business configuration comprehensively. It includes all business configuration data, organizational structures, and reference content in the parallel line workspace.

During branching, when you enable the Copy Master Data for Business Configuration Consistency option, not only configuration master data but an extensive list of configuration and master data tables are transferred to ensure consistency. This comprehensive data transfer is critical for maintaining configuration integrity across both lines. Be aware that business users are copied without business role assignments, so you’ll need to assign business roles after branching. Also note that the development and test systems in the main line will be temporarily locked during the branch process.

Step 2: Configure Wave 2 Countries/Regions in the Parallel Line

In your parallel line, configure the second group of countries/regions (wave 2). Limit this to a maximum of 5 countries/regions to avoid performance degradation and activation issues during the subsequent merge.

In SAP Central Business Configuration, scope the new countries/regions, configure organizational structures such as company codes, plants, and sales areas, and set up country/region-specific customization and localizations. Utilize the Configuration Localization Tool (CLT) if applicable for your implementation.

During wave 2 configuration in the parallel line, limit main line activities to minor adjustments and customer adaptations only. Avoid large configuration changes in the main line to prevent complex merge conflicts later. This discipline is critical for maintaining a clean separation between lines.

Step 3: Test and Validate in the Parallel Line

Transport your configuration from the parallel customizing tenant (D.120) to the parallel test tenant (T.120) using the Export Customizing Transports and Import Collection apps. Conduct thorough system integration testing (SIT), user acceptance testing (UAT), and end-to-end business process validation.

Use the test automation tool in your parallel test tenant to streamline testing activities. For more information, refer to Testing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Step 4: Rebase from Main Line

Before merging wave 2 into the main line, perform a rebase to synchronize any incremental configuration changes made in the main line since the branch or last rebase. Regular rebasing ensures your parallel line includes urgent fixes, legal updates, or small adjustments made in your productive main line.

In SAP Central Business Configuration:

  • Initiate a rebase from the workspace for the parallel line.
  • Review possible scoping or configuration conflicts. For more information, refer to Conflict Resolution.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition:

  • Review and activate the Customer staging entries in the Display Business Configuration Stagings app.
  • Resolve any conflicts using the compare & adjust tool in the Implementation Activities app.

Step 5: Run Pre-Merge Validation

Before initiating a merge, run the Simulation of Cross-Client Validation check in the Manage Check Runs for Business Configuration app. For more information, refer to Simulation of Cross-Client Validation.

Step 6: Merge Wave 2 to Main Line

Once validation is clean and the rebase is complete, initiate a merge from the SAP Central Business Configuration workspace in the parallel line. All wave 2 configurations, including organizational structures and related product configuration, are transferred to the SAP Central Business Configuration workspace of the main line, deployed to the customizing tenant (D.100), and immediately activated there.

The merge process includes:

  • SAP Central Business Configuration staging entries
  • Customer staging entries

Activate the Customer staging entries to complete the transfer to the main line. For more information, refer to Maintain Business Configuration Stagings.

Step 7: Verify Deployment and Activation in the Customizing Tenant (D.100)

Check that the wave 2 configuration has been successfully deployed from the SAP Central Business Configuration workspace for the main line to the customizing tenant (D.100) of the development system in the main line and activated there. Review the staging status in the Display Business Configuration Stagings app.

Step 8: Transport to Test System

Move the wave 2 configuration from the customizing tenant (D.100) to the test tenant (T.100) for comprehensive testing before forwarding it to production.

Step 9: Adaptations in the Main Line

Perform any necessary product configuration for wave 2 directly in the customizing tenant (D.100). Transport these refinements to the test tenant (T.100) for final validation. This ensures that all final adjustments are captured in your production path.

Step 10: Deploy to Production

At this stage, you have two options:

  • Immediate Go-Live: If wave 2 is ready for production, transport the full configuration to the production tenant (P.100) for cutover and go-live activities.
  • Staged Approach: Keep the validated wave 2 configuration on hold in the test tenant T.100 and merge it with subsequent waves for consolidated testing before moving to production. This provides flexibility to go live with multiple waves together rather than deploying each wave individually.

Wave 3 and Beyond: Repeat the Cycle

When you’re ready for wave 3 and beyond, you have two options depending on your organizational preferences and project requirements:

 

Aspect

Option A: Clean Slate Approach

Option B: Continuous Extension

Process

Delete the existing parallel line and request to branch from the main line again

Continue using the same parallel line

Configuration

Set up a fresh parallel line and configure the next 5 countries/regions

Add up to 5 more countries/regions to the existing parallel line

Best For

Large-scale implementations requiring strict governance

Smaller rollouts where team familiarity with existing structure adds efficiency

Advantages

Cleaner tenant environment, simplified governance, reduced complexity

Continuity, efficiency if team knows the existing structure

Recommended For

Implementations with many waves (for example, 40+ countries/regions)

Short-term extensions with limited additional waves

The choice between these options depends on your preference for tenant cleanliness, organizational complexity, and the total number of remaining countries/regions to implement.

Example: Implementing 40 Countries Using the Merge Strategy

To illustrate how the 5-country/region limit works in practice across a large-scale implementation, consider a scenario where you need to implement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition across 40 countries/regions. Using the merge strategy, you would structure your implementation as follows:

  • Wave 1: Foundation (Countries 1-5)
    Implement the first 5 countries/regions directly in the main line. Complete configuration, testing, and validation. You can either go live with these countries immediately as your baseline or keep them in the test system for consolidated deployment with subsequent waves.
  • Wave 2: First Parallel Line Use (Countries 6-10)
    After wave 1 is stable, branch the main line and configure the next 5 countries/regions in the parallel line. Test thoroughly in the parallel test tenant (T.120,) rebase to synchronize with any main line changes, then merge back to the main line. After successful testing in test tenant (T.100), decide whether to deploy to the production tenant (P.100) immediately or consolidate with additional waves before go-live.
  • Wave 3: Clean Slate (Countries 11-15)
    Delete the existing parallel line and branch again from the main line (which now contains 10 countries/regions). Configure the next 5 countries/regions in the fresh parallel line, then follow the same rebase, merge, and test process.
  • Waves 4-8: Repeat the Cycle (Countries 16-40)
    Continue with this pattern: delete the parallel line after each successful merge, branch again, configure the next batch of 5 countries/regions, and merge. You maintain flexibility to go live with selected wave groupings rather than deploying each wave individually to production. Each wave builds on the consolidated configuration in the main line, ensuring a single source of truth while respecting the 5-country/region merge limit.

Timeline Considerations

When planning your multi-wave implementation, keep the following timing factors in mind:

  • Each wave requires time for branching (8-10 hours typical), configuration, testing, rebase, merge, and deployment
  • Plan for adequate time between waves to ensure stability and allow for thorough validation
  • The clean slate approach (Option A from Wave 3 onwards) is recommended for large-scale implementations to maintain system cleanliness and reduce complexity

This systematic approach allows you to scale to any number of countries/regions while maintaining the integrity and performance benefits that the 5-country/region limit provides.

Best Practices for Success

Several key practices will significantly improve your success with parallel line implementations:

  • Sequential Wave Implementation: After completing one wave of up to 5 countries/regions, you can immediately begin planning and configuring the next wave. There’s no waiting period required between waves, which means you can maintain project momentum while respecting the technical constraints.
  • Main Line Discipline: During parallel line configuration periods, keep main line changes minimal to simplify rebase and merge operations. This discipline prevents configuration conflicts and makes synchronization between lines much smoother. Reserve your main line for urgent fixes and critical updates only while major implementation work happens in the parallel line.
  • Thorough Testing: Always validate configurations in the test tenant (T.100) or parallel test tenant (T.120) before moving to production. Never skip testing phases, even when you’re confident in your configuration. The test phase catches issues before they impact production and provides a safe environment for end-to-end process validation.
  • Regular Rebasing: Before every merge, perform a rebase to ensure your parallel line includes the latest main line updates. This practice minimizes merge conflicts and ensures consistency between your configuration tracks.
  • Clear Wave Planning: Define your country/region groupings upfront to optimize the parallel line lifecycle. Understanding which countries/regions will be implemented together, and in what sequence, allows you to plan resources, manage stakeholder expectations, and structure your project timeline realistically. Remember that the 5-country/region limit is absolute for merge operations, so plan your waves accordingly.

Conclusion

The merge strategy provides centralized governance and a single source of truth for your global implementation while leveraging the isolation and safety that the parallel line provides during configuration and testing. This transforms complex multi-country/region rollouts from overwhelming challenges into manageable, systematic processes that can be repeated reliably across multiple implementation waves.

To execute this strategy successfully, focus on the following critical practices:

  • Understanding the 5-country/region deployment limit as a strict per-wave constraint when merging configurations back to the main line
  • Branching strategically to create isolated workspaces for new country/region groups
  • Configuring efficiently within the 5-country/region limit to optimize performance
  • Rebasing regularly to stay synchronized with main line updates
  • Merging carefully after thorough validation to maintain configuration integrity
  • Minimizing main line changes during parallel line configuration periods
  • Defining country/region groupings upfront to plan resources and timelines realistically
  • Validating thoroughly in the parallel test tenant (T.120) before merging and again in the test tenant (T.100) before production deployment

By following these practices and respecting the technical constraints, organizations can scale globally while maintaining system performance and configuration quality.

Have you used the parallel line for multi-country/region implementations? I’d love to hear about your experiences and any lessons learned in the comments below!

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Strategy Adobe Forms On-Premise
entry.content:
Saritha, the go-to-solution for RISE is SAP forms Service by Adobe! You'll find all the information here: SAP Discovery Center Service - Forms Service by Adobe

 

Regards,

Sandra

entry.title: Export to Excel without opening Excel
entry.content:
Hello,

We use SAP and the GUI 8 PL8.

I want to switch all my users over to the online versions of Google and Microsoft Office 365 —so that I no longer need any local installations on the client. Currently, Excel opens automatically when a download from SAP with a “.xls” extension is initiated.
How can I configure SAP or the GUI so that it only downloads the Excel file without launching Excel afterward? My goal is for the download to go directly to the cloud via the GDrive or OneDrive tool, so that colleagues can then open the Excel file from there.

Greetings

entry.title: SAP TM - delivery cretion with bom and deliver group
entry.content:
Hallo,

We create outbound deliveries from the freight order. The freight unit contains the subitems of a bill of material (BOM), which also have delivery groups assigned.

Now we want to adjust the quantities before creating the delivery. If we reduce the quantity of one item in a BOM (for example, by half), all other subitems of that BOM are also reduced proportionally in the delivery. The remaining quantity is then assigned to a new freight unit.

However, this behavior only occurs when BOMs have delivery groups. Without delivery groups, it is possible to adjust only a single subitem as desired.

entry.title: Re: Explore SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, CDS Views on SAP Business Accelerator Hub
entry.content: Thanks. This is a very well technially explained document for SAP Enthusiast, specially for data consultants of SAP. Are there any Inventory related CDS views in SAP S/4 HANA? Is there any CDS view that is an alternate of report MB5B so I  am also able to extract its data into BW or DSP?

entry.title: Re: How to track DCD status history and time spent in eac...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Credit Management, the log you see in UKM_CASE is part of the Case Management framework. Here’s how the data is technically stored and linked:

1) Status Change Log
The entries in the Log tab are stored in SRMPROTOCOL (protocol table for case management). Each change of attribute/status is logged here with timestamp, user, old/new values.

2) Case Attributes
The documented credit decision (DCD) itself is stored in UKM_DCD_ATTR and linked to the case object in SCMG_T_CASE_ATTR.

3) Link to Sales Order
The DCD object references the sales order via UKM_DCDOBJ (object assignment table). This is how you connect case number → DCD → sales order.

4) Custom Statuses
If you have enhanced the approval flow with custom statuses, they are still logged in SRMPROTOCOL. There is no separate “history” table for custom statuses; the log is the authoritative source.

Recommended technical approach

1) Primary Source
Use SRMPROTOCOL joined with SCMG_T_CASE_ATTR to extract the chronology of status changes for each case.

Linking:

  • Case number (GUID) → SCMG_T_CASE_ATTR
  • Case GUID → UKM_DCD_ATTR (credit decision attributes)
  • UKM_DCD_ATTR → UKM_DCDOBJ (sales order linkage)

2) Time in Status Calculation
Read SRMPROTOCOL entries for status changes. Sort by timestamp. Calculate duration between successive status changes.

3) CDS View / Reporting
Since there is no delivered CDS for “time in status,” you will need a custom CDS view or ABAP report that joins SRMPROTOCOL with the case/DCD tables and computes elapsed time.

entry.title: Biometric Authentication using Passkey with SAP BTP SDK for Windows
entry.content:
This article demonstrates how to implement Passkey-based authentication in SAP BTP SDK for Windows applications, integrated with SAP Cloud Identity Services.

First, I will describe the necessary configuration to be performed by an administrator in:

  • BTP subaccount
  • IAS
  • Windows OS

Admin - Configuration in SAP BTP

Create a subscription for (1) SAP Mobile Services and (2) SAP Cloud Identity Service.

Then, establish trust between your BTP subaccount and SAP Cloud Identity Services and configured a custom identity provider for applications.

Admin - Configuration in SAP Cloud Identity Services

Enable Biometric Authentication for the XSUAA app used by SAP Mobile Services.

Please also enable Biometric Authentication for the User Profile self-service offered by IAS.

Create a Passkey on Windows 

Prerequisites for Creating a Windows Passkey

  1. Operating System: Windows 10 (version 1903 or later) or Windows 11
  2. Hardware Requirements:
    • TPM 2.0 (Trusted Platform Module) support
    • Biometric device (fingerprint reader or IR camera for facial recognition) or PIN support
  3. Windows Hello Enabled: At least one of PIN, fingerprint, or facial recognition must be set up

Create Passkey via Windows Settings

Step 1: Open Sign-in Options

  1. Click Start Menu → Settings (or use shortcut Win + I)
  2. Select Accounts → Sign-in options

Step 2: Set Up Windows Hello

If you haven't set up Windows Hello yet, complete one of the following options first:

Table

Verification Method

Setup Steps

PIN

Click "PIN (Windows Hello)" → "Add" → Enter and confirm your PIN

Fingerprint

Click "Fingerprint recognition" → "Set up" → Follow prompts to touch fingerprint sensor multiple times

Facial Recognition

Click "Facial recognition" → "Set up" → Face the camera to complete scanning

Step3：Activate a device for Biometric Authentication

On the windows device where you tested on, access “Profile Management ” page in web browser and https address is like “https://<host url of this custom IDP>/ui/protected/profilemanagement”, focus on "Biometric Authentication" section, click Add button to register current device and use biometric to create passkey for this custom IDP. Then this passkey will be saved

Use saved passkey to do authentication via the custom Identity Provider

Create or use an existing application which security type is OAuth on SAP Mobile Services(Cloud Foundry) cockpit. Then, in authconfig.json client project, set correct options as below.

Also please set correct public API Key in App.xaml

Please make sure that the authentication page opened in OS browser, as biometric authentication didn’t work in webview2. You can find details in this wiki: Auth Extension & Customization - SAP BTP Windows SDK - Wiki@SAP

Finally when the user launch Windows BTP SDK application and move to choose the custom identity provider. The IdP website from IAS is presented. The user can choose biometric authentication because the admin allowed this form of authentication for the XSUAA. The rest of the mobile app's onboarding steps will be executed until onboarding is completed, and the user will see the business content.

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community! My name is Moshe Naveh and I'm really excited about the new journey ahead o
entry.content: @Sanjay-mn  Great post! Thanks a lot for joining:)! Looking forward to seeing more of your posts on the SAP Community

entry.title: API to GET RFQ PDF using RFQ number in S4 Hana public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

We have created an Iflow in SAP Integration suite where we are using API_RFQ_PROCESS_SRV to fetch the RFQ data from S4 hana public cloud system. We want to fetch the RFQ PDF using the RFQ number which is not available in this API, unlike for Purchase Order I can fetch using API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV/GetPDF. Kindly suggest the API to fetch the RFQ PDF in Iflow from S4 hana public cloud.

Regards,

Hinal

Created
1. VisualAPP
2. TargetApp

entry.title: Re: DOX: Fields now identified that does not exist in schema
entry.content: Thanks Thomas for sharing the issue. This is not expected. We are checking internally on this and get back to you asap. Meanwhile, It would be greatly appreciated if you can create a ticket and share the schema and document for us to replicate the specific issue. Thanks

entry.title: Create Outgoing Invoice - Custom fields S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I get that the question 'can custom fields be added to Create Outgoing Invoices' a ton of times, but most of the KBAs have been deleted, or the previous responses contradict with what is currently happening in my clients system. 

There are 6 custom fields present in the Coding Block Context, 5 of these are displayed in the item table in Create Outgoing Invoices - there is a previous question with an answer stating 5 is the maximum number of custom fields displayed (the KBA link states the document has been deleted) - Solved: Is it possible to add Custom Fields in "Create Inc... - SAP Community.

Is there any way of manipulating which 5 of the 6 fields are displayed? There are no GUI specific UIs for Coding Block

Is there still a limit of 5 fields?

Does the app still support custom fields? - Later KBAs have stated that there is no support for custom fields at all, but this seems to be inaccurate as fields are visible and are available for input.

Can anyone confirm exactly what is possible and what is not possible?

Thanks

Tom

entry.title: Another new addition to the SAP Community Forum
